headline: Mastercard to Buy Stablecoin Infrastructure Firm BVNK for Up to $1.8 Billion
summary: Mastercard on Tuesday said the acquisition, which includes $300 million in contingent payments, expands the Purchase, N.Y., payment giant’s support of digital assets and value movement across currencies, payment rails and regions.  Mastercard and Coinbase were both circling BVNK last year before Coinbase called off talks to buy the London-Based firm, according to several published reports.

headline: Oil Relief Opens the Door for AI to Take Back the Tape
summary: Monday’s rebound on Wall Street looked encouraging on the surface, but beneath it was still the same fragile market story: investors remain glued to every move in oil and the Strait of Hormuz, while eagerly rotating back into AI-linked names the moment energy pressure eases. Lower crude gave the market room to breathe, but not yet a reason to relax.

headline: Is Aon’s Stablecoin Premium Deal Quietly Reframing Coinbase’s Role in Real-World Payments Infrastructure (COIN)?
summary: Aon plc recently completed what it describes as the first stablecoin insurance premium payments among major global brokers, using U.S. dollar-backed stablecoins like USDC on Ethereum and PayPal USD on Solana to settle premiums for clients including Coinbase and Paxos. This move highlights Coinbase’s growing role in regulated stablecoin infrastructure, as insurance payments begin to use the same blockchain rails that underpin its stablecoin and payments business. We’ll now examine how this...
